zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye於1487年7月12日出生在英國德比夏爾哈登 霍爾時，她的父親Sir John Pye52歲，母親Anne Brydges41歲。她於1508年4月與John Donington在英國米德萊可斯托克 聶文頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1552年2月24日在英國英格蘭德比夏爾貝克韋爾_ 希丘特享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可亨格拉福聖 約翰 拉特藍恩斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye于1487年7月12日出生在英国德比夏爾哈登 霍爾时，她的父亲Sir John Pye52岁，母亲Anne Brydges41岁。她于1508年4月与John Donington在英国米德萊可斯托克 聶文頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1552年2月24日在英国英格兰德比夏爾貝克韋爾_ 希丘特享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可亨格拉福聖 約翰 拉特藍恩斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Pye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (north-West Midlands, Lancashire, Yorkshire, and Norfolk): nickname from Middle English (Old French) pie ‘magpie’, which could be given to someone who wore black and white clothing, had grey-streaked dark hair, or had a loud, chattering voice and impertinent behaviour, or was cunning or sly. The uncomplimentary senses derive from the bird's behavior and are recorded uses of the word in Middle English and early Modern English.

English: occasionally perhaps a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named Pie ‘magpie’. There were two London taverns so named, but whether these gave rise to a hereditary surname is not known. Surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English pie ‘merciful, compassionate, kind’, a variant of Old French, Anglo-Norman French piu, peu; see Pew (2).

variants.name: Pew
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French pi(u), piw, peu ‘holy, pious’, also ‘merciful, kind, compassionate’.

variants.name: Pyatt
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire): nickname either from Middle English piot ‘magpie’ (Old French pïot, a diminutive of Old French pie), commonly used to denote a chattering woman, or alternatively from an unrecorded Middle English pyard, an uncomplimentary term derived from the same Old French word + -(h)ard. Compare Pye .
Variant of   Piatt  , a surname of French and Walloon origin.
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with the variant Piatt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Pie
meanings: English: variant of   Pye  , in part a cognate of 2 below.
French: nickname from Old French pie ‘magpie’, perhaps given to a talkative person or someone who had a loud, chattering voice.
French (Pié): variant of Piet ‘foot’. Compare Pease and Pee .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name from any of various places in northern France called Courtenay, the original meaning of which is ‘domain of Curtius’. However, from an early period it was wrongly taken as a nickname derived from Old French court nez ‘short nose’. In the U.K. it is found chiefly as a girl's name.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye was born on 12 July 1487, in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, her father, Sir John Pye, was 52 and her mother, Anne Brydges, was 41. She married John Donington in April 1508, in Stoke Newington, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 24 February 1552, in Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John Donington en avril 1508, à Stoke Newington, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 24 février 1552, à Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye am 12. Juli 1487 in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Pye 52 und ihre Mutter Anne Brydges 41. Sie hat im April 1508 in Stoke Newington, Middlesex, England John Donington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 24. Februar 1552 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye è nata il il 12 luglio 1487 nel Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Sir John Pye, aveva 52 anni e sua madre, Anne Brydges, aveva 41 anni. Lei sposato John Donington nel aprile 1508, nel Stoke Newington, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 24 febbraio 1552, nel Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pyeが1487年7月12日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアハッドオン ハルで生まれた時，父Sir John Pyeは52歳，母Anne Brydgesは41歳であった。1508年4月にイギリスミッドレセックスストク ニューイントンでJohn Doningtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1552年2月24日にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアベイケウエル シルクウイトで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクヘングラブスト ジョン ラテラン'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Stoke Newington St Matthias, Middlesex,: 영국 미들레세스 스토크 뉴잉턴 스트 맷히아스
ko.Church of the Reconciliation, Hengrave,: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 헨그레이브 스트 존 레이터아'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye(이)가 1487년 7월 12일 영국 더비실르 하던 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Pye(은)는 52세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Brydges(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1508년 4월 영국 미들레세스 스토크 뉴잉턴에서 John Donington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1552년 2월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 베이케웰 시르쿠이에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 헨그레이브 스트 존 레이터아'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.12 July 1487–24 February 1552: 12 de julho de 1487–24 de fevereiro de 1552
pt.Stoke Newington St Matthias, Middlesex,: Stoke Newington St Matthias, Middlesex, Inglaterra
pt.Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, England, U: Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Church of the Reconciliation, Hengrave,: St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, United: Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye nasceu em 12 de julho de 1487, em Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Pye, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Anne Brydges, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com John Donington em abril de 1508, em Stoke Newington, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 24 de fevereiro de 1552, em Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Stoke Newington St Matthias, Middlesex,: сток нюингтон ст мэттяс, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.Church of the Reconciliation, Hengrave,: ст джон лэитиран'с чурч, хенграв, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye родилась 12 июля 1487 г. в местности хаддон холл, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Pye было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Brydges, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Donington апрель 1508 г. в местности сток нюингтон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 24 февраля 1552 г. в местности беикюелл сиркуит, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности ст джон лэитиран'с чурч, хенграв, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Courtenay Pye nació el 12 de julio de 1487, en Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Pye, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Anne Brydges, tenía 41 años. Se casó con John Donington en abril de 1508, en Stoke Newington, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 24 de febrero de 1552, en Bakewell Circuit, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en St John Lateran's Church, Hengrave, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
